# ADR <NNNN>: <short decision title>

## Status

Proposed | Accepted | Superseded by ADR <NNNN>

`Proposed` records a design candidate only. It does not authorize
implementation. An `Accepted` status requires the applicable Adjudicator
architecture or technology-selection approval and should link its follow-up
Issue(s) when implementation is required.

## Context

<What forces or requirements make this decision necessary? What is currently
ambiguous or unresolved?>

## Dependency Adoption Evidence

Use this section when the ADR selects or materially depends on a library,
framework package, provider SDK, datastore client, build tool, or test helper.
Write "Not applicable" when no dependency is being selected.

- Vulnerability/advisory check:
- Version-specific examples checked:
- Troubleshooting and known-issue evidence:
- Minimal real-file test or fixture plan:
- POC feasibility/result:
- Clean Architecture boundary:
